pi_items.source: Sygic official documentation (sygic.com/what-is/vehicle-profiles) shows consumer Sygic Aura vehicle profiles include only: Profile Name, Fuel Type, Emission Category, Production Year. Maximum speed is documented for Sygic Truck & Caravan Navigation (com.sygic.truck) and Professional Navigation, but NOT for consumer Sygic GPS Navigation (com.sygic.aura)
Specified in task.json description and metadata field. Task explicitly states 'exactly 90 km/h'

Sygic supports metric (km/h), imperial (mph), and US units for vehicle parameters. For Truck Navigation documentation confirms: 'You can use metric, imperial, or U.S. units when setting these parameters'. km/h is the metric standard for speed measurements in Sygic
Task setup script specifies package 'com.sygic.aura' which is the consumer Sygic GPS Navigation app, not the Truck & Caravan variant

pi_items.verified_value: Vehicle profile settings exist with configurable parameters including fuel type, emission category, and production year
pi_items.source: Sygic official documentation confirms vehicle profiles are available in Sygic GPS Navigation. Source: https://www.sygic.com/what-is/vehicle-profiles

privileged_info_summary: The task targets Sygic GPS Navigation (com.sygic.aura), the consumer navigation app. According to Sygic's official documentation, vehicle profiles in the consumer app support configurable parameters including fuel type, emission category, production year, and vehicle name. The target value of 90 and unit of km/h are documented in the task specification. However, 'Maximum speed' is not documented as a configurable vehicle profile parameter in the consumer Sygic Aura app - it is only documented as a vehicle parameter in Sygic Truck & Caravan Navigation and Sygic Professional Navigation variants.
